septal
  • API reference
  • CasADi schema
  • View page source

CasADi schema

Standard NLP schema for septal.

Defines the input problem (NLPProblem) and output result (SolveResult) dataclasses used across all solver backends.

NLP standard form:

min f(x) s.t. lhs <= g(x) <= rhs

lb <= x <= ub

class septal.casadax.schema.NLPProblem(objective, bounds, constraints=None, constraint_lhs=None, constraint_rhs=None, n_decision=None, n_starts=5)[source]

Bases: object

Describes a nonlinear programme in standard form.

Parameters:

  • objective (Callable) – JAX function f(x) -> scalar (or CasADi-compatible callback). Will be wrapped for the chosen backend automatically.

  • bounds (List[Any]) – [lb, ub] — list/tuple of two JAX/NumPy arrays, each of shape (n_decision,).

  • constraints (Callable | None) – Optional JAX function g(x) -> (n_g,) vector. Paired with constraint_lhs / constraint_rhs to form lhs <= g(x) <= rhs.

  • constraint_lhs (Any | None) – Lower bound on g(x); shape (n_g,) or (n_g, 1). -inf entries are unconstrained from below.

  • constraint_rhs (Any | None) – Upper bound on g(x); shape (n_g,) or (n_g, 1). Typically 0 for inequality constraints g(x) <= 0.

  • n_decision (int | None) – Dimension of x. Inferred from bounds if not supplied.

  • n_starts (int) – Number of multi-start initial guesses to generate / evaluate.

bounds: List[Any]
constraint_lhs: Any | None = None
constraint_rhs: Any | None = None
constraints: Callable | None = None
property has_constraints: bool
property lb: Any
n_decision: int | None = None
n_starts: int = 5
objective: Callable
property ub: Any
class septal.casadax.schema.SolveResult(success, objective, decision_variables, constraints=None, message='', timing=0.0, n_solves=0)[source]

Bases: object

Result returned by every septal solver.

Parameters:

  • success (bool) – True if the solver converged to a (locally) optimal feasible point.

  • objective (Any) – Optimal objective value f(x*).

  • decision_variables (Any) – Optimal primal solution x*.

  • constraints (Any | None) – Value of g(x*); None when the problem has no constraints.

  • message (str) – Solver status string (e.g. IPOPT’s return_status).

  • timing (float) – Wall-clock time spent in the solver (seconds).

  • n_solves (int) – Number of multi-start solves that returned feasible solutions.

constraints: Any | None = None
decision_variables: Any
message: str = ''
n_solves: int = 0
objective: Any
success: bool
timing: float = 0.0
